rows.notes: Clear header text. No amounts expected for structural label.

Amount shown as '9.16.6' on right margin. Duplicate entry (same as row 4). Clear handwriting and valid currency values. Consistent alignment with previous entries.

Amount shown as '50.15.4' on right margin, preceded by underline indicating a subtotal. Handwriting is relatively clear. Currency values are valid (shillings 15, pence 4). Verification: summing rows 3–6 would produce a subtotal row; this aligns with standard ledger structure.

rows.description: Vale of the Fund for Adorozion in the 3d Cent reduced
rows.notes: Structural heading text. Handwriting is cursive and archaic; 'Adorozion' and 'Cent' are difficult to read with certainty, but appear to be account descriptions. No amounts present. This introduces a new account section.

rows.description: Amount of worth Wos Account 276. 18. 7 with reared Dw: 66. 17. 6 [split]

The figure shown as '99.8.10' is legible but context is unclear. Pence value of 10 is valid (0–11). This row appears to combine multiple monetary references.

Amount shown as '25.17.5' on right margin. Text appears to read 'maund' (possibly French 'main' or an abbreviation), suggesting a subtotal or intermediate balance. Handwriting is faded and cursive. Currency values are valid. Row structure suggests a calculation result.

rows.description: N.D Dividend on 34. 15. 7 not yet invested.
rows.notes: No amount visible on right margin for this row. Text is legible (N.D = Not Dividend; note details an account reference '34.15.7'). This appears to be a sub-item or explanatory entry without its own monetary amount. Ink is somewhat faded but readable.
